facts.text: Edison’s industrial research laboratory, called Edison’s “invention factory,” opened in 1876 and put the township on the map for global innovation.
The township’s Bonhamtown area on Old Post Road honors Nicholas Bonham, a freeholder from 1682–83.
Edison was originally incorporated as Raritan Township in 1870 before adopting its current name in 1954 to avoid confusion with other Raritan towns.
The first settlers arrived in the late 1600s when the area was part of Woodbridge and Piscataway, making Edison one of New Jersey’s oldest communities.
The Pennsylvania Railroad’s arrival in the late 19th century turned Edison into a rail hub, spurring rapid growth and industrialization.
The Edison Memorial Tower, listed on the National Register of Historic Places, was illuminated in 1986 with 12 period street lamps to celebrate Christie Street’s historic lighting.
